name: Persistent authentication session with state serialization
description: Verifies that the skill produces a correct persistent auth session implementation with state serialization, restoration, and expiration handling.
input: Implement a persistent browser session that logs into a web application (email + password form), saves the authentication state to a JSON file, and restores it on subsequent runs. If the saved session has expired, re-authenticate automatically. The app uses HttpOnly cookies for session management.

assertions.description: Must use Playwright's storageState API to serialize cookies including HttpOnly cookies that manual extraction would miss
Must check for existing state file before deciding whether to perform fresh login or restore saved state
Must include error handling for expired sessions, corrupt state files, or failed restoration attempts
Must use accessible locators for the login form interaction rather than CSS selectors
Must serialize state to JSON format for persistence across runs

name: Multi-tab state management with shared context
description: Verifies that the skill correctly implements multi-tab browser sessions where tabs share authentication state but maintain independent navigation, with proper cleanup to prevent memory leaks.
input: Create a multi-tab Playwright session where 3 tabs share the same authenticated browser context. Tab 1 monitors a dashboard, tab 2 processes a queue of items, and tab 3 collects logs. Tabs share cookies but navigate independently. Include memory management to prevent leaks from long-running tab usage.

assertions.description: Must use context.newPage() to create multiple tabs within the same browser context for cookie sharing
Must operate within a single browser context so that all tabs share cookies and storage
Must include page/resource cleanup logic to prevent memory leaks in long-running sessions
Must use async patterns for coordinating independent tab operations without blocking
Must manage multiple page references with distinct roles (monitor, processor, logger)
